history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: IgA deposits in the dermal papillae are characteristic findings typically associated with **dermatitis herpetiformis** (DH). DH is an autoimmune condition closely linked to celiac disease, where IgA antibodies against transglutaminase are deposited primarily within the dermal papillary layer of the skin, leading to blistering lesions. Answer: A.
